site.title: Johannesburg Nature-based Solutions Impact Dashboard
site.tagline: Communicating the impact of nature based solutions in Johannesburg
lang.label: Language
hero.title: Restoring the Jukskei, one intervention at a time
hero.lede: This dashboard shows what nature based solutions are being delivered along the Upper Jukskei River catchment from the inner city through Lorentzville, Bez Valley, and Alexandra, and how they reduce flood risk, cool neighbourhoods, improve the environment, and create local opportunity.
hero.note: Minimum viable product. Prepared for the City of Johannesburg, IISD, and the SUNCASA partnership.
context.title: The SUNCASA programme at a glance
context.note: Programme wide targets for the Upper Jukskei catchment. Site level values elsewhere on this dashboard are illustrative and await the SUNCASA data catalogue.
pillars.title: Four themes of impact
pillars.subtitle: Each theme carries a small set of clear indicators drawn from the SUNCASA Performance Management Framework and Environmental Management Program.
indicator.proposed: Proposed indicator, to be confirmed during Inception
indicator.noTrend: Trend view available once the SUNCASA data catalogue is confirmed.
codesign.title: Shaped with you, not for you
codesign.body: This prototype is a starting point for the Inception Phase. Indicators marked as proposed are options for discussion. Stakeholder feedback from the City of Johannesburg, IISD, and community partners will decide the final set.
map.title: Where the work is happening
map.caption: Illustrative intervention sites along the Jukskei corridor from the inner city through Bez Valley, Queen's Wetland, and Alexandra. Exact geometries will be confirmed from the SUNCASA data catalogue.
stories.title: Stories from the river
stories.subtitle: Short, clearly labelled community stories that sit alongside the numbers.
story.label: Community story
data.title: About the data
data.body: The dashboard uses existing SUNCASA monitoring data and selected public sources. No new data was collected. Values shown in this prototype are illustrative and await the SUNCASA data catalogue.
footer.owner: Hosted and owned by the City of Johannesburg Environment and Infrastructure Services Department
footer.project: SUNCASA, Scaling Urban Nature based Solutions for Climate Adaptation in Sub Saharan Africa
footer.partners: Led by IISD and the World Resources Institute, funded by Global Affairs Canada, with the City of Johannesburg, JCPZ, JICP, Water for the Future, Women for Climate Justice Southern Africa (GenderCC SA), Zutari, the Alexandra Water Warriors, and the Johannesburg Road Agency
footer.rights: Full ownership of code, configuration, and data rests with the City of Johannesburg.
story.photo: Photo to be supplied by the City
data.meta.title: Indicator definitions and metadata
data.meta.body: Every indicator follows the SUNCASA indicator model: a plain language name, a reason it matters, the latest value, a source, an update frequency, and clear caveats.
data.sources.title: Data sources
data.sources.body: SUNCASA Performance Management Framework and Environmental Management Program, complemented by selected public city and partner data sets where readily available.
data.limitations.title: Limitations
data.limitations.body: Sample values are illustrative pending the SUNCASA data catalogue. No real time feeds, no predictive modelling, and no new data collection are part of this MVP.
data.update.title: Update approach
data.update.body: Data is prepared in CSV or Excel and ingested into the dashboard at build time, keeping data, presentation, and content fully separated for easy maintenance.
meth.title: Methodology and approach
meth.lede: This page summarises how the MVP is built and how it is designed to scale into a city wide platform without a full rebuild.
meth.principles.title: Design principles
meth.principles.1: Communication first, not an internal analytics tool.
meth.principles.2: Indicator driven and narrative supported, inspired by MyPeg.
meth.principles.3: Plain language, high contrast, and WCAG aware.
meth.principles.4: Mobile and low bandwidth friendly by default.
meth.principles.5: Data, presentation, and content kept separate.
meth.roadmap.title: Scalability roadmap
meth.phase1.label: Phase 1
meth.phase1.title: SUNCASA MVP
meth.phase1.body: Jukskei intervention areas, curated indicators, static and lightly interactive visuals, EN and ZU content structure.
meth.phase2.label: Phase 2
meth.phase2.title: Enhanced SUNCASA
meth.phase2.body: More indicators, richer stories, optional filters, and Power BI or Azure data connections where the City wants them.
meth.phase3.label: Phase 3
meth.phase3.title: City wide NbS platform
meth.phase3.body: Expanded indicator catalogue across Johannesburg, automated translation, comparisons, and a strategic fundraising function.
meth.oos.title: Explicitly out of scope for the MVP
meth.oos.2: No advanced analytics or machine learning
meth.oos.4: No city wide coverage in this phase
map.fallback: Interactive map requires JavaScript. Intervention sites along the Jukskei corridor from the inner city through Bez Valley, Queen's Wetland, and Alexandra include cleared reaches, rehabilitated riverbanks, tree planting zones, and riparian restoration areas, all confirmed from the SUNCASA data catalogue.
pillar.chart.title: Progress over time
pillar.proposed.title: Proposed additions
pillar.proposed.body: These indicators are options for discussion during the Inception Phase. Their inclusion depends on data availability and stakeholder priorities.
story.disclaimer: Narrative and translation to be supplied by IISD and the City of Johannesburg.
story.1.title: Turning trash into treasure
story.1.excerpt: Waste pulled from the Jukskei is reworked into public art by local artists in Alexandra, turning cleanup into pride.
story.2.title: The Water Warriors
story.2.excerpt: A community crew clears debris and restores the banks, earning income while protecting homes from floods.
story.3.title: Shade for every street
story.3.excerpt: Trees planted near low income households bring cooling and greenery to blocks that had little of either.
story.4.title: Litter traps become river art
story.4.excerpt: Handwoven traps catch waste before it floods Alexandra, while artist Hannelie Coetzee and the Water Warriors turn the debris into sculpture.
404.title: Page not found
404.body: The page you are looking for is not part of this MVP dashboard.
